task_completion.description: Access the HEC-RAS plan HDF file for the Muncie model.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a Python script (e.g., using h5py), an HDF viewer application, or a terminal command targeting the file ending in .p04.hdf or .p04.tmp.hdf in the /home/ga/Documents/hec_ras_projects/Muncie/ directory.

task_completion.description: Identify the upstream and downstream cross-section stations and the reach length.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for console output, script variables, or a data viewer showing the most upstream station (15696.24), downstream station (237.6455), and reach length (15458.6 ft).

task_completion.description: Identify the peak flows and times of peak for the upstream and downstream stations.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for console output, script variables, or a data viewer showing the peak flows (~21000.0 cfs upstream, ~20998.4 cfs downstream) and times of peak (15.00 hrs upstream, 20.17 hrs downstream).

task_completion.description: Calculate the flood wave travel time and average wave celerity.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a calculator app, script execution, or console output showing the calculation of travel time (difference in peak times, ~5.17 hrs) and average wave celerity (reach length divided by travel time in seconds, ~0.83 ft/s).

task_completion.description: Save the results to the specified text file with all required labels.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor or terminal output (e.g., using 'cat') showing the final 'flood_wave_travel_time.txt' file in /home/ga/Documents/hec_ras_results/. The file must contain all 9 required labels (Upstream Station, Downstream Station, Reach Length, Peak Flows, Times of Peak, Travel Time, Average Wave Celerity) with their corresponding computed values.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent actually extracted data from the HDF file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent must use a script, tool, or viewer to read the .hdf file. It should not magically produce the correct values without evidence of inspecting the file contents.

integrity.description: Verify that any script used performs actual extraction and calculation.
integrity.visual_evidence: If a Python or bash script is used, it should contain logic to read the HDF file and compute the differences, rather than just containing print statements with hardcoded final answers.

integrity.description: Verify the output file is saved in the correct directory.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file path in the text editor or terminal must explicitly show /home/ga/Documents/hec_ras_results/flood_wave_travel_time.txt, not just a local or temporary directory.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The true values for the Muncie model are: Upstream Station: 15696.24, Downstream Station: 237.6455, Reach Length: 15458.6 ft, Upstream Peak Flow: 21000.0 cfs at 15.00 hrs, Downstream Peak Flow: 20998.4 cfs at 20.17 hrs, Travel Time: 5.17 hrs, Average Wave Celerity: 0.83 ft/s. The output file must be saved to /home/ga/Documents/hec_ras_results/flood_wave_travel_time.txt.
